name: Jordanian Arabic (Ammani)
notes: Jordanian Arabic ‚ÄĒ Ammani koine, a new dialect formed from Jordanian + urban Palestinian contact (Al-Wer 2020). INPUT CONTRACT: fully-diacritized Arabic. Ŕā qaf is a socially reallocated variable: […°] (traditional Jordanian, much male speech) alongside [ Ē] (urban Palestinian / the emerging Ammani prestige ‚ÄĒ third-generation women use [ Ē] consistently, men use both: Al-Wer 2020:560). Both are listed, …° first as the distinctively Jordanian value. ō¨ jim ‚Üí [d í]~[ í] (men vary, women [ í]: Al-Wer 2020:560). Interdentals: the Ammani koine favours the merged stops [t d dň§] (Al-Wer 2020:560; Fadda 2016:27-28 ‚ÄĒ the koine consonant system is 'identical to other Levantine cities'); the interdental variants persist in traditional Jordanian/Bedouin speech and remain in the inventory via the parent. Vowels /i u a/ + /iňź uňź eňź oňź aňź/ and /aj aw/‚Üí[eňź oňź] (bajt‚Üíbeňźt, sň§awt‚Üísň§oňźtň§: Fadda 2016:30) are inherited from the Levantine parent.

sources.author: Al-Wer, E.
sources.title: New-dialect formation: The Amman dialect
sources.publisher: In: Lucas & Manfredi (eds.), Arabic and contact-induced change, 551-566. Berlin: Language Science Press
sources.notes: READ in full. Amman koine: (q) is a socially reallocated variable, […°] (Jordanian) ~ [ Ē] (urban Palestinian); gen-III women use [ Ē] consistently, men both (p.560). («ß) jim: [d í]~[ í], women [ í] (p.560). Interdentals: both genders favour stop variants (p.560).

sources.author: Fadda, H.
sources.title: Language Variation in Western Amman
sources.publisher: MA thesis, Canada Institute of Linguistics (CanIL)
sources.notes: READ (phonology chapter). Ammani koine consonants = urban Palestinian [ Ē t d dň§  í], 'identical to other Levantine cities' (pp.27-28). Qaf labels (Al-Wer & Herin 2011:60): [g] gilit, [q] qeltu, [ Ē] Jerusalem, [k] rural West Bank. Vowels: short /i u a/, long /iňź uňź eňź oňź aňź/ (Al-Wer 2011:4). Diphthongs /aj aw/‚Üí[eňź oňź] except before glide: bajt‚Üíbeňźt, sň§awt‚Üísň§oňźtň§ (p.30).

sources.author: Almbark, R. & Hellmuth, S.
sources.title: Acoustic analysis of the Syrian Arabic vowel system
sources.notes: READ in full. Damascus/Syrian vowel system: five long /iňź eňź aňź oňź uňź/, three short contrastive /i a u/; mid short [e o] and schwa are allophones of /i u/ (¬ß3-4). Mid-long /eňź oňź/ from /aj aw/ coalescence (¬ß1.1). /aňź/ higher/fronter in Coastal Syria than Damascus (p.2).

sources.author: Cowell, M.W.
sources.title: A Reference Grammar of Syrian Arabic (Based on the Dialect of Damascus)
sources.notes: Standard Damascus grammar. Primary text NOT obtained (Internet Archive borrow-only, no full text); its Damascene vowel classification (5 long / 5 short + schwa) is cited here only as reported and tested by Almbark & Hellmuth 2015 ¬ß1.3.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for South Levantine Arabic.
